title: Internet blackout simulation update — day 11
description: 2026-03-10 update: simulated internet blackout losses put United States first, China second, with world exposure at $38.79B/day.
excerpt: 2026-03-10 / day 11: a same-length local internet blackout simulation for the world and top economies, with United States and China leading the modeled daily losses and the world scenario at $38.79B/day.
body_paragraphs: This post does not say these economies are currently losing money because Iran is offline. It uses Iran's blackout duration as a time clock and simulates what a same-length domestic internet blackout would cost each economy on day 11.
Under the world scenario, a blackout of this length implies $38.79B in daily GDP loss and $371.26B in cumulative loss since day 1.
For the large-economy scenarios, the biggest simulated daily losses today are: United States: $11.34B/day; China: $6.09B/day; Germany: $1.65B/day.
Method: 2025 nominal GDP baseline × country-specific digital sensitivity coefficient × an outage-duration escalation curve that gets harsher as the blackout continues.

signal.summary: Fallback signal used because no live signal source was available during build.

author: Pamin
model_note: This package does not estimate how much Iran's blackout is hurting other economies right now. Instead, it uses Iran's outage duration as the clock and simulates what a same-length local internet blackout would cost for the world scenario and for each top-10 economy using a 2025 GDP baseline plus a day-by-day escalation curve. A second shock overlay models the first-wave disruption premium caused by the outage itself; it starts large, then decays with time.
